text: The patient is a forty-year-old woman, gravida one and para one. She was referred to the gynecologist with a history of multiple fibroids and menorrhagia with severe anemia. Hemoglobin was down to six point four grams and the patient had received four units of blood. Ultrasound of the pelvis showed a large fibroid uterus with three fibroid tumors. An MRI confirmed the ultrasound findings. The MRI showed two submucosal myomas, five centimeter and two centimeter in size. It also showed a five centimeter intramural degenerating myoma.

The patient requested a myomectomy and wants to have the option of having children in the future. The patient continued to receive iron infusions. Lupron therapy was not initiated because of concern of lack of plane for dissection and enucleation of the myoma. The patient was brought in for a robot-assisted laparoscopy with possible laparotomy. Liomyoma, tubes, ovaries, and ureters were identified.
Vasopressin is injected into the posterior leiomyoma. An incision is made over the myoma site, revealing the capsule, but no clear plane for enucleation is visible.

The surgeon continues dissection, draining necrotic material from the degenerating myoma, which is then suctioned. A suture is placed on the myoma for tagging, facilitating future removal. The base of the myoma is coagulated and cut, separating it from the underlying myometrium.

The surgeon identifies the second myoma, makes an incision, and identifies the capsule. Enucleation is performed, followed by placement of a suture. The base of the myoma is cut and coagulated, then separated from the underlying endometrium and myometrium. The same procedure is repeated for the third myoma.

The surgeon identifies the endometrium and approximates it using a 2-0 Vicryl suture. The myomectomy incision is then closed with a 2-0 VLAC suture in two layers, ensuring proper closure of the surgical site.
The surgical team successfully completes three myomectomies, removing two submucosal myomas and one intramural myoma through a single uterine incision.
